channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon? — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:34 UTC

OP

You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62125227
>>62124756
they will get turned away, even if the crowd lined up at the current levels! there will only be 21 million coins and many people have more than one already. the crowds will be turned away since there is not enough of them left. i read that there are already something like 200k bitcoin holders and only a million coins left to be mined. even if each of those new coins went to a new holder (they won't) the maximum number of people that can have a bitcoin at the same time is 1.2 million.

62125283
I placed an order last weekend and initiated an ACH transfer to buy more tomorrow.
t. lucky retard

62125298
>>62125283
>lucky retard
well, half of that statement is true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125672
>>62125310
It won't dump until July.

62125766
>>62125310
Selling is for fags. Just buy weekly and hold until it hits a million. If it fails (it clearly won’t) then oh well. You lost a gamble.

62125827
>>62125582

>it’s not decentralized
There are 70,000+ nodes worldwide enforcing bitcoins rules. Unless you think 1 guy secretly owns them all?

>its not anonymous
Good luck getting widespread adoption and ETFs for your monero

>it succeeded because it became more centralized
You have this backwards.

I wish you luck with your altcoins but your post is incoherent. It’s bad because it became more centralized while other coins are good for becoming more centralized?

62126111
>>62125583
you think more than 1.2 million people can own a bitcoin at the same time?

the supply is capped, there is simply not enough of them for the crowd. maybe they will be able to rent one for a time but by and large the people that are going to have a bitcoin already do.

62126137
>people like OP turned bullish at the pico top of the bear flag

yikes

62126166
>>62126137
Zoom out nigger. We aren’t selling til $5m

62126217
>>62124756
I picked xrp because $1 -> $1 million is a million x whereas $100k -> $1 million is only 10x

62126224
>had my buy orders at $63k
>dip barely clears $65k
Fuck I'm pretty sure I didn't miss the boat but this price action is demoralizing.

62126314
Why people would rush to buy Bitcoin at 1 mi when they didn't at 100k?

62126338
>>62126314
kek

if we even reach 200K next bullrun consider yourself very lucky

62126414
MY STRATEGIC BITCOIN RESERVE NOW HOLDS 0.22997 BTC!!!!!!

62126672
>>62126314
Protip, people are fucking retarded.

62126806
>>62126111
They can own Bitcoin, they may not own A bitcoin. It's only relevant if you're a math retard.

62126809
>>62126672
>>62126314
Why did people rush to buy Bitcoin at $10k when they didn't at $100?

62126824
i will never understand contemporary bitcoin shit
there are only 21,000,000 bitcoins in existence right?
if bitcoin hit $1,000,000 that would mean the bitcoin market cap would be $21 trillion
compare that to market caps of other industries rn, i don't see how it's physically possible

it seems like people desperately want to believe they can just fucking do what people did with bitcoin in the early 2010s and make a ridiculous amount of money just by uhhh... buying it again and waiting for it to happen again
stupid though

62126828
correct me if i'm wrong about any of that, i want to understand

62126833
is it all a joke?

62126984
>>62125310
I'm thinking of selling but I moht wait a little longer. This pump certainly won't last long though, so getting the timing right will be tough.

62126986
>>62125582
Even if all of this was truth, who cares? It can still make me money.

62126990
>>62126986
wtf is that supposed to mean
i mean, anything CAN make you money

62127238
>>62126824
Idk man it might still hit 300k at least which will hopefully gigapump my alts.

62127252
>>62127238
fair enough i guess

62127263
>>62126314
at $1m they will need it

62127315
>>62124756

62127745
>>62126414
well done, anon

62127808
>>62126824
You’re almost there anon. You’re on the precipice of figuring it out. Bitcoin will be the number one store of value in the world. Buy it before that happens.

62127809
>>62126809
it wasnt easy to buy at 100

62127812
>>62126824
/thread. new buyers are effn late.

62127822
>>62127812
If it’s lower than the MC of gold, which is an inferior store of value in every way, then you’re actually early.

62130754
>>62126824
>i will never understand contemporary bitcoin shit
>there are only 21,000,000 bitcoins in existence right?
the bitcoin is valuable because 21 argument is so low iq
and there's less than 21m in existence because of data loss

item.title: CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP — 56 replies — 93.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:11 UTC

OP

CLEAN IT UP WAGIE

CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Early replies (first 3)

62119436
>>62119165
legend

62119443
Some people have almost no power over anything in their life. They do trivial evil acts like this because its their only thing they can do. Stealing stationary from work is a similar thing. His life is probably miserable.

62119490
>>62119443
Put the fries in the bag, wagie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124173
>>62124064
Oh, I thought it was something else. Guess I need to visit the optometrist.

62124245
>>62119165
it would be worth a firing to punch him

62124743
Wagie revenge fantasy.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uiCnpzQ1Tew

62125214
testy=

62125676
Clean it up wagie

62126971
>>62120744
IA shit. No women would dare to walk alone in India or Indian-like countries

62127025
>>62119494
Target is the best place to pick up chicks friend. Bitches go crazy for that place.

62127104
>>62122207
>>62124064
Oh no, a little ketchup is gonna get smeared on the table if I move it to the edge and use another cup or bucket to catch the liquid. The horror

62127130
>>62119165
i'd just sweep really obnoxiously in his face if he did that, not fucking sit there and take a picture and rant about it on my phone

62127159
>>62127130
That’s why you’re a wagie.

62127166
>>62127159
i don't have a job

62127182
>>62121287
This dude lose his job and get jail time

62127186
>>62127182
The wagie? lmaooo

62127189
>>62119165
n word behavior honestly

62127194
this thread is retarded

62128020
>>62127194
Hurry up with my order, wagie.

62128023
>>62128020
>>62124173
>>62123792
infini necrobump glownigger retard thread
look at the timestamps

62128026
i already told you i don't have a job
if this weren't a retard thread you'd have come up with something funnier but it's blatant vapid spam
this site is fucked

62128031
avoiding flamewarring with me to keep the post count lower & thereby keep the thread up for longer
like i said this site is fucked, everyone is way too self-aware and there are shills around every corner and the mod team treats it like a top-down rat maze with no engagement

62128032
>>62123557
here's another one

62128038
>>62119165

62128047
i'm tempted to just fill the thread with replies & see what happens

62128050
it would take hours & there'd likely be duplicate spam threads on the board within 48 hours though
makes you wonder what the mods are doing, or at least it should
>>62123557
>>62119165

62128085
>>62119443
kek

62128168
>>62128038
if you'd done a shitty mspaint trace job it would still have a soul, like pic related

62128292
>>62119165
>be me
>restaurant wagie in singapoor
>some mutt tourist think its funny to throw ketchup on the ground
>find mop and tell him to clean up
>hahaha no you are slave you do it
>call police
>he starts going off about first amendment and rights
>police gets agitatef because he doesnt do as they tell him, he argues like typical american
>they grab him to take into police car
>he starts fighting back, yelling about rights
>they taze him and beat him with police sticks
>take him to jail
I was kekking the entire time

62128310
>>62127025
Target and Trader Joe’s are can’t miss.

62130589
>>62125676
How did she manage to smuggle all that onto the flight?

62130590
>>62128292
Cope wagie. You still had to clean it up.

62130710
This thread reminded me that Kek Adams has passed.

item.title: Best peptide stock, Bachem BANB — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Best peptide stock, Bachem BANB

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:32 UTC

OP

We just had a 5% dip. So good moment to take a look.

Part 1 is here:

>>62098490


NO ONE, literally NO ONE, is talking about this stock. not on reddit, nor on X.

and I urge you to verify everything i say, but it will take you max 10 minutes.

assuming you are bullish on peptides (gray, black and white market). this is the BEST instrument to express this bullishness.

brief facts:

1) marketcap 5B
2) profitable, pays dividend, swiss
3) founding family owns ~58% of the shares
4) supplies to Novo, Eli Lily, etc
5) didn't pump yet

30%+ EBITDA margins, 35-45% expected sales growth in 2026

if you are bullish on peptides, this is a no-brainer. ive seen many people unfortunately buying HIMS, Hong-Kong listed scams or even solana tokens to express their peptide thesis. no bueno.

I'm long 50,000 shares

Early replies (first 3)

62122951
>>62122950
im in. its happening.

62122953
>buying stocks

62123048
I am intrigued anon. reading your last post, may respond in ten to fifteen min or so

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62123777
>>62122950
>>62100230
>BCHMY
Oh what the fuck it's been buyable on Charles Schwab less than a month. Buying a couple hundred shares tomorrow.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bachem_Holding

62123794
Also OP's xeeter account from the previous thread got heemed.

62125340
Only these 2 options, no vol on both almost

62126995
buy the swiss listing. get a normal brokerage. ibkr is goated

62127982
>>62126995
>international buck-breakers

62128081
not available on my broker

62128837
What about BCHMY? I see that on Fidelity.

62129753
>>62128837
Yeah that's the alternate ticker for people buying via the US market.

62129763
>>62122950
Why would I be bullish on peptides? They're not even legal in Europe.

62130404
FirstWave capital just went long BANB this week. The CEO has 500,000 followers and just mentioned Bachem in an interview yesterday.

He does look like a douche tho

62130424
>>62130404
Is it worth getting a new brokerage account just to buy BANB directly rather than BCMHY? I looked back at the chart and it's half fucking gray days it's so illiquid.

62130487
>>62130424
IBKR is worth it regardless. They pay 4% on USD. Are safer than banks and the fees for trading are the lowest while offering the widest universe of products.

For bachem only the ADR is fine if you do <50k a week. Liquidity will improve coming months imho. Just put orders at Swiss price corrected for FX. Don't need to take out the wide spread

62130491
>>62122953
what's the alternative?

62130647
>>62130487
The ADR is also not 1:1, each share is 0.1 shares of BANB. So 500M ADR shares outstanding = 50M shares of BANB outstanding on US markets.

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1968378/000119380523000298/e618343_f6ef-bh.htm

62130653
>>62130487
>>62130647
Explain this stuff to me as if I were a drooling oaf. I’m a bit lost.

62130715
>>62130653
You should always buy stocks on their home exchange to avoid any potential downsides.
IBKR is the best global brokerage around offering almost the whole world.

62130725
it doesn't show up on my robinhood how do i buy it

62130748
>>62122950
What's the appeal?
It looks like dogshit

item.title: >tfw parents have no saving >or 401k >or investments so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant… — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw parents have no saving >or 401k >or investments so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:40 UTC

OP

>tfw parents have no saving
>or 401k
>or investments

so um what the FUCK am i supposed to do when they get old and cant work lmao. I'm going to have to take care of my own family and kids + myself + their care and life + saving for my own retirement???? HOW???????

Early replies (first 3)

62130654
just close your eyes and pretend they don't exist

62130656
>>62130650
If they couldn't do it during the easiest time, it will be less likely that you will do it.

62130657
>>62130650
the gov will give them rental assistance for a shitty apartment in a bad area of town. they will be poor and live off social security.

this happened to a divorced guy i knew. she took his house and half his 401k. then he lost his job at 60 and couldnt get another one. he "died" a few years later.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62130684
>>62130650
this >>62130654

or work harder/acquire more wealth

in the past, peoples parents usually came to live with a family member once they got too old
if you put them in a nursing home they'll get sedated and beaten up by black people and watch TV so keep that inmind

it's actually not that hard/expensive to take care of them, just let them live with you & feed them
u don't have to pay the medical jew and probably shouldn't

62130687
>>62130657
>this happened to a divorced guy i knew. she took his house and half his 401k. then he lost his job at 60 and couldnt get another one. he "died" a few years later.
RIP

i hate the antichrist

62130691
they'll probably go on medicaid and/or social security disability. low-end assisted living, free clinics, what this guy said >>62130657
naturally they'll depend on you more to get by month-to-month. no fancy gated community or premium caretakers

62130695
Get on social security and move to central/south america

62130696
>>62130687
yeah he was really a nice guy. he lived next-door to us growing up. good luck to everyone bros

62130697
>>62130650
pull yourself up by the bootstraps sonny boy. a real man like myself worked a part time job to buy a Miami beach house for my parents to retire comfortably in.

62130722
dunno. live in a trailer park. that's cheap as fuck.

62130729
>>62130696
>good luck to everyone bros
likewise anon
>>62130722
honestly might not be a bad idea
i have cousins who live in a mobile home area outside a city in texas, they're poor but it's comfy as hell, no problems with neighbors etc

62130730
>>62130650
My dad died and I lost money because I had to pay about 5k for funeral costs and to have his apartment cleaned. He had no savings, was 66

62130750
With about 250k rough net worth, including retirement money by 30, you likely won't even need inheritance to survive.
Whats youre excuse?

62130761
>>62130750
>youre

62130772
>>62130761
Thsi inst docisrd, fgagot. I cna splel hevower i wnat.

item.title: Another day another hack — 20 replies — 52.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another day another hack

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:24 UTC

OP

KelpDAO for $280M+, with AAVE catching some collateral damage

https://x.com/nftmufettisi/status/2045580862301831619

Early replies (first 3)

62129664
>>62129662
the future of finance

62129670
>>62129662
real talk is it worth getting into crypto hacking/draining wallets?

it cant be THAT hard right

62129671
Maybe its just the devs cashing out

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62129715
>>62129662

62129734
>>62129662
Didn't Chainlink ccip prevent this?

62129750
>>62129734
Layerzero bridge hack, maybe they will learn that you should just use Chainlink

62129757
Thank God.

62129781
>>62129670
Thinking the same now that we have ai that can hack goverments. One good move and you are set for life.

62129899
wow layer zero huh
man, if only there was some kind of alternative, more secure solution...

62129933
>>62129670
Yes. Lot's of faulty smart contracts and L1s to exploit.

62129937
Defi might be cooked. It never existed. They could always Freeze your funds even if there was no hack. Bullish for BTC, unironically.

Shitcoin factories have to die

62130445
>>62129670
>it cant be THAT hard right
The issue is you cant learn that skill anywhere, since hackers will not publish a textbook step by step instructive on how to replicate their work. Also, AI will not help you on that because of guardrails. So basically your gatekeeper is to be an skilled computer scientist with vast blockchain knowledge. There is no workaround.

62130452
>>62129781
pls show me the AI that hacks governments

62130545
>>62130445
You don't have to know the details of each exploit, you just need knowledge of blockchains, networks and reverse engineering.
Many CVEs and previous exploits are well documented.
LLMs do help you if you don't specifically mention what you're up to and use them indirectly like checking for potential vulnerabilities in "your" code.

62130551
>>62130545
>you just need knowledge of blockchains, networks and reverse engineering.
JUST, lmao.

62130600
idgi, why is there 280mil to steal from a project using 1/1 dvn but link can't pump above $10?

62130610
Don't forget that it's literally a crime, as if you robbed a bank. If you will be found you're finished. You will spend a long time in jail, and your CS career is done.

62130628
>>62130610
>CS career is done
Who needs a career when you have $280m?

62130676
>>62130600
Link is probably the reason these hacks are occurring in the first place. I bet that fat piece of shit is rugging every single project he can.

62130732
>>62129937
>Bullish for BTC, unironically.
>Shitcoin factories have to die
Things will never revert to Bitcoin only. Bitcoin is not going to supplant fiat, you are delusional. There will always be shitcoins now, in some capacity.

item.title: /biz/ schizos needed — 6 replies — 15.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/biz/ schizos needed

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-19 03:23 UTC

OP

>The Economic Defense Unit (EDU) is a new Pentagon organization established in early 2026 under the second Trump administration, designed to integrate economic tools and leverage directly into U.S. military planning, operations, and national security strategy—essentially treating economic power as a core element of defense and great-power competition.

>Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg issued a memo in April 2026 formally creating the EDU and the role of its director. The unit reports to Feinberg and serves as the main hub for "fusing economic leverage and requirements into joint U.S. military planning and operations." It acts as his principal advisor on economic competition across the Department of War.

>This team helps oversee and deploy up to $200 billion in defense-related investments over three years. The goal is to use private capital, equity stakes, loans, and deals to strengthen the U.S. defense industrial base, counter supply chain vulnerabilities, and support national security priorities.
How do we profit off this? There must be some way to follow the money and see where they're shoving money.

Early replies (first 3)

62129531
Nevermind i asked the AI

The Economic Defense Unit (EDU) and Office of Strategic Capital (OSC) are directing funds into several publicly traded companies via equity stakes, convertible securities, loans, and production deals.Direct Investments:L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) — $1 billion convertible preferred investment in its Missile Solutions / solid rocket motor business. The unit will spin off into a new public company via IPO in the second half of 2026, with the government converting into equity.

reuters.com

MP Materials (NYSE: MP) — ~$400 million in convertible preferred stock + warrants (giving the U.S. government ~15% stake as largest shareholder), plus a $150 million loan and long-term offtake agreement for rare earth magnets and materials.

mpmaterials.com

Broader Beneficiaries:Major primes receiving expanded production contracts and demand include:Lockheed Martin (LMT)
RTX (RTX)

Smaller/mid-cap names in drones, electronics, and components (such as AeroVironment or Leonardo DRS) are also seeing related contract flows.These deals focus on scaling munitions, critical minerals, and supply-chain resilience. The EDU is still ramping up, so more announcements are expected.

62129532
Economic power was always a core element of great power competition
The UK controlling the world reserve currency was how they beat the much more powerful Napoleon
money beats guns

62129550
>>62129531
AeroVironment (AVAV) — Strongest upside potential. Moderate to Strong Buy, with average targets indicating 50–65%+ upside. Drone demand surge (a major EDU focus area) drives this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62129764
bump
>>62129550
yeah these niggas good
i didn't know hte company was public xd

62129767
the oculus guy seems to be getting involved with military, but i don't know if there's a way to profit from that

62130731
>>62129531
>MP Materials
i hate bagholding this dogshit company

item.title: Assuming you have a remote job making $120k/year with $175k saved, what would you do? — 13 replies — 15.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Assuming you have a remote job making $120k/year with $175k saved, what would you do?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-19 02:56 UTC

OP

Assuming you have a remote job making $120k/year with $175k saved, what would you do?

A) Build skills to get a better remote job (or a second one) - Medium effort, but could risk working way more

B) Build a remote small business/side hustle to increase income to hedge against a poor job market - High effort, but likely less effort over time and more predictable income

C) Just be patient with the markets, dca the near bottom of BTC/MSTR, sell on the way up + travel for fun - Low effort, relies on luck (have done it before with a small chunk last cycle, hoping history rhymes)

D) index funds + travel for fun - Low effort, probably will never make it though

Early replies (first 3)

62129245
>>62129234
Do you have your home paid off? What's your mortgage rate or are you renting?

62129250
i would be on a beautiful 40+ acre property living in a fuckin truck with starlink while i develop the property and doing the exact same strategy as i am now, except i keep way more money and get to work on my shit while i work.

62129254
>>62129245
Just renting since I want maximum liquidity. Only $900 in a LCOL spot in USA. Debating leaving this spot to stay a few months a time in other countries though. From what I've seen it can be roughly the same cost. That or might stay and just go on trips instead every quarter.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62129289
>>62129234
How old are you?

62129300
>>62129234
B, buy a property in Mexico/peru

62129305
>>62129234
i'd take a nice vacation, for 1-2 months if possible, then try B
whilst trying to keep the saved money growing modestly, or at least not losing it to inflation

if you can get a source of income which doesn't eat your time (and this should be possible with that much capital), then you should be set

62129327
>>62129289
29
>>62129305
> this should be possible with that much capital
what kind of thing would you recommend with the capital I've got? I was thinking starting very small, then scaling after I break even

62129421
>>62129234
I would invest in my video game and become a millionaire

62129437
>>62129327
>I was thinking starting very small, then scaling after I break even
that's probably the way to do it
keep in mind you'll probably burn through some money experimenting to see what works, as well
>what kind of thing would you recommend with the capital I've got?
not sure honestly, but try to think of it like a very open-ended engineering problem with constraints
like if u want to do it mostly from your bedroom, that's a constraint
if you only want to make money from a business which doesn't directly interface much with the general public, that's another constraint
then try to come up with ideas from there

62129439
constraints could also include shipping laws in your country, or other things outside of your control
but those may or may not be relevant to whatever idea you come up with
just think things through

62129765
>>62129437
Thanks, yeah the only thing I can think is something related to marketing.
I could find people already making money and essentially help them make more by marketing more effectively on social media/reddit targeting their exact customers etc, then take commision. Besides this probably some vibe coding thing but that also require marketing, probably by making youtube video tutorials funneling to the software. I've seen channels do this exact this making 6 figures by charging $5-20 for a one time charge and getting 10k+ people to buy coming from the youtube channel or their reddit etc. I think usually they take something open source and just improve the UI.
Besides this I guess ebay could be an option, but then I'd have to bank on inventory. Haven't seen proof of concepts for anything else.

62130625
>>62129234
If your job can be done remotely, it can be done by an AI or an Indian.

62130690
>>62129234
You are on /biz/. Literally start trading at a professional level. It doesn't even have to be day trading, just do research like an autist for months per trade.

item.title: Something is coming soon — 17 replies — 12.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-19 02:24 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

62125665
>>62125647

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

62124590

62125560

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62128644

62128731
>>62128247
Too red eye, bless up rassclat. Yuns nuh wut da papa be singing! Ya no call dem ‘gaters long mouths till ya pass dem. We booyaka Babylon in due tim mon. Big up ya self natty dreads, when chicken marry, hawk dey near. But I be a broken record. They ai dat web3 wooooooo, faze me. Jah guide.

62129571
>>62128731

62129610

item.content:encoded:
The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse This pump is comp…

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-04-18 21:56 UTC

OP

The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse

This pump is completely artificial and designed to trap as many plebs as humanely possible

Early replies (first 3)

62120115
open the fuckin strait

62120217
>>62120101
10.000 kbbls/d oil is missing, fertilizer even worse. People going to start walking somewhere if they are hungry and no relive in sight.

62120280
>>62120101
Why not just nuke Iran and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62122036
>>62121556
bbls = blue barrels

Standard Oil was the biggest game in town for a long time, and their oil barrels were painted blue, and rapidly became the standard barrel size in the industry. Oil barrels of that size became known as "blue barrels" whether they were actual blue-painted Standard Oil barrels or not.

62122395
>>62121326

62122517
Brer Trump punched the Tar Baby. Comical shit.

62122624
>>62120291
>i know basic news everyone knows
>i know less than investment banks, hedge funds, governments

Banks and NPCs: famous for being correct about everything and never being blinded by greed.

62122745
>>62120101
Bear gf

62122816
>>62122745
Bear wife

62122824
>>62120101
well my option expires in less than 24 hours so it won't matter

62122860
i wish a hot girl would hug me

62122901
bobina is a fucking WHORE

62122909
>>62120291
>>62122624
you write like women

62122948
>>62120101
I'm always astounded by how beloved Bobina is by this board but that Mumette, Crabitha, and Swanica are all rarely used comparatively.

62122959
Yeah but yknow, if I sold right now, I'd win.

62123008
>>62121505
>>62121508
>>62121520
what in tarnation
i still don't understand what happened with the previous short squeeze a few years ago, or what a short squeeze is

62123162
>>62122948
its literally one guy posting it, so he can subtly shill his coin.

62123172
>>62120101
i would pay $2m to be bobo in this pic

62123206
>>62120101
>humanely possible
>humanely
>human

62123257
>>62123008
>someone sees something is poised to do badly, think "this things fucked 100%", bets on it doing badly by going to somebody with shares, telling them to sell today and give them the money from the sale, and in X days they will come back and give them the exact amount of shares they had sold for them (you want them to be cheaper to buy back so you make money)
>The thing in question potentially doesn't get fucked, and infact goes higher than the price it was sold at for them
>Puts the short seller in a shitty position to where they either have to put more money up to close their short sale via either closing and buying the shares at the higher price, or having to put up more collateral while paying interest to keep the short open in the hopes that it swings back in their favor (what that previous short squeeze you eluded to continues to do and continues to obfuscate)
>Is very bad when the short sale in question is done in excess of both the short sellers funds (leverage) and the amount of shares that are available (float) / exist (outstanding shares)
>worse still if/when the entity facilitating this transaction calls you and says "give me my fucking money" (margin call), because they themselves are broke because multiple other entities are doing the exact same thing to them and theyre technically liable for moving "the bag" around (and they can be margin called by their lines of credit)
>further compounded by whoever gets sold the shares then turns around and lets somebody else short them off their hands and so on and so forth ad infimum
>sometimes there aren't even any shares to begin with and they sell off phantom shares that you think you own and are real but are just complete bullshit because the person selling them to you couldn't find it / were unable to borrow it
>This happens all day everyday across every stock and every market while the dtcc (the central counterparty that has to pay everybody out) does absolutely fuck all
Hope this helps!

62123339
>>62123008
When you go short, you MUST buy at some point to close the position.
When you short a stonk, you're borrowing the stonk from someone else and immediately selling at current price. You have to re-buy that stonk at some point and return it to the lender (to close the position).
When you open a short position in futures, you are obligating yourself to sell something for <x> dollars a pop. You need to have the thing in the first place in order to sell it (duh). So if you don't have it to sell, you'd have to buy it in order to fulfill your obligation to sell.
You might not think of it that way, but that is what's happening, mechanically.
Ok.
A short squeeze is when the market looks at something that's massively shorted. The market sees this and says, "If we push price high enough, those shorts will all be stopped out and forced to BUY at a loss to close their position."

When BYND rallied last year, part of the motive was that there were so many shorts on BYND that "if we can hit their stop losses, it will trigger a colossal series of market buy orders."
When silver rallied earlier this year, part of the motive was that "Comex has shorted more silver than it can afford to sell IF price were high enough."
When GME happened so many years ago, part of the motive was "GME is massively shorted, and we want to hit their stops."

So America has been shorting oil to keep price down. Tamping it, as /pmg/ faggots say. This comes with a caveat: eventually America will have to execute all these short contracts. And if oil price is up bigly, oil will be short squeezed.
Hope this make some sense at all.

62123352
>>62123257
>>62123339
ty, ty

62123353
>>62123008
>>62123339
In short (HA HA), a short squeeze is a stop hunt directed at short positions.

62123357
Bobo, where my hug?

62123361
>>62120280
Why not just nuke Israel and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

62123369
>>62120115
We don't need the strait

62123378
>>62123206

>>62122901

62124116
>>62120101
I dont care.

62124225
>>62120280

62126020
>>62124225

62127029

62128823

forcing a beta of an established meme is cringe

62130030
>>62128823

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 69 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-04-18 18:27 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121692
75$ in two years and 5 months

62121723
>>62121668
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
That's great that you pointed that out and at the same time every company sooner or later goes under. There does not exist a company in history of mankind that survived the test of time. No name was ever big enough to sustain itself through time. The only thing that never changes is the fact that everything always changes.

>Plus Amazon has billions of dollars to address their flaws
That's true and the thing is that no amount of money can solve centralized server uptime. There's always something. If you have a system as complex as AWS that's run across multiple countries and relies of tens if not hundreds of millions of lines of code then there's ALWAYS going to be a flaw. It's impossible to have a system as big as AWS, that runs of centralized servers, and that it works all the time. No matter how much money they spent, no matter how many smart engineers they throw at it... The only solution is a Web3 cloud. Can Amazon pivot into a Web3 cloud? Sure. They can literally just use DOT as the underlying infrastructure. DOT is NOT against Amazon. It can compliment it. Amazon can just simply bend the knee and use DOT as their compute provider, right? If Amazon doesn't follow the trend then sooner or later people will abandon it. Why would you use a more expensive cloud provider that has less up time? There is NO point. I as a CS myself know this for a fact. You earlier said
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
To be honest? If I can get a cloud provider for cheaper and have them have more uptime I'd switch in a heartbeat. The same rules don't apply in computers/programming than they do in other sectors. People who are into computers move faster than other sectors, because we're taught BY DEFAULT that things constantly change and never stay the same. If you're paying 5% more and the uptime is 5% less then you're literally shooting yourself in the foot.

62121726
75$ end of race

62121749
>>62121668
What I'm trying to say is that CS is the fastest moving sector for a reason. And the reason is that people in the sector love adopting new technology at a fast pace. If I can get something for 5% cheaper with 5% more uptime and I have the time to migrate my service and it's not too much of a hastle then I'd do it in a heartbeat. Sure if the migration would be EXTREMELY TEDIOUS then I might reconsider. It's just, normally it's not and takes maybe a day or two. Imagine your whole company paying 5% less while your customers would be 5% happier with your service. You'd be stupid not to take that deal, right? Centralized servers ARE EXPENSIVE in nature. Decentralized servers are INEXPENSIVE. Why? Well centralized stuff needs upkeep while decentralized stuff does not. Look at Acurast (https://acurast.com/). They're literally a mobile phone cloud provider. How much does the upkeep for your mobile phone cost? 0$. Do you need janitors and cleaning personnel? Nope. Do you need engineers to run your phone? Nope. Do you see what I mean? The price margin for 1GHz of computing power provided by a mobile device is probably 100x lower than 1GHz of computing power provided by a centralized server. You would switch cloud providers if they offered a 100x lower price while providing a 100% uptime, right? You wouldn't be like "Oh, but their NAME is not AMAZON. OMG OMG. Let's not use this because they have no NAME RECOGNITION. I'd rather pay 100x more and have less uptime than switch. OMG OMG OMG". Do you notice how that sounds once I put it into perspective? Sounds awkward, right?

>>62121692
>>62121726
Soon we shall catapult into the heavens of nirvana.

62121769
>>62121723
> every company goes under
Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
Amazon created quite the monopoly in the retail sector. Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.

>AWS pivots to Web3 cloud
Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?

62121787
>>62121749
Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS

Who still owns the OS space?

Exactly

62121817
>>62121787
>Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS
Great that you point that out. That's an apples to oranges comparison. Linux isn't meant for the average user. It's meant for programmers in a lot of ways. We're also not talking about OS. Are we?

>>62121769
>Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
True, and no company survives in it's original form. Everyone adapts to the time. And the time is shifting towards a Web3 cloud.

>Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.
True, and I'm not saying that. What I'm saying is that Web3 will, over time, take a large share away from it, due to it being cheaper and having more uptime. Again, centralized servers CAN NOT compete with decentralized ones on uptime and cost. And those two factors are literally the only two important factors when it comes to a cloud provider. It has to be cheap and have the largest possible uptime.

>Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?
The thing is that Amazon will just use Web3 clouds. I doubt they'll build their own because they'd have to spent a lot of money to re-invent the wheel. DOT is already ready in a lot of ways. This year JAM hits and that'll transform it into a TRUE Web3 cloud capable of handling the load of AWS. Look, I'm not saying that DOT will IMMEDIATELY kill everyone. It'll be a slow transition and more and more companies will be switching. You know the 20/80 rule? It takes as much time to go from 0% to 20% than it does from 20% to 80%. This was true for smart phones, the internet, cars, ... So, if it took 10 years to go from 0% users to 20% of the population using something then it'll take that same amount of time to go from 20% to 80%. TO BE CONTINUED

62121830
>>62121769
Copied from Google AI

As of early 2026, over 650 million to nearly 1 billion people worldwide are estimated to own or use cryptocurrency. Growth is driven by high adoption in regions like Asia and Africa, with about 6.8% of the global population holding digital assets. While ownership is high, active monthly users are estimated lower, around 30–60 million, indicating a large population of passive holders.

Projected Growth: The number of users is expected to reach 993 million by 2026, with user penetration expected to reach 12.63% of the population, according to 1.
Regional Leaders: Asia dominates with 43% of global users, followed by North America (17%) and Europe (15%).
Top Adoption Countries: India, Nigeria, and Indonesia show high adoption rates, often driven by local currency instability, according to 4 and 3.
User Demographics: Approximately 60% of crypto users are under the age of 34, with a majority being male.

Active on-chain usage continues to rise as mobile wallet installations grow, with over 1.1 billion installations tracked as of early 2026.


We're almost at the 20% mark. BTC was created 17 years ago. So, we'll be at the 20% mark by like 2028 to 2030. So like 19 to 21 years. So, by the year 2050 80% of the world population will be using Web3.

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: UHHH OIL BAGGIES THIS WASN'T PART OF THE PLAN?!?!? — 36 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
UHHH OIL BAGGIES THIS WASN'T PART OF THE PLAN?!?!?

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-04-18 19:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62124467
smelly doom posters btfo

62124521
SAY ... HIS ... NAME

62124524
/biz/antium in SHAMBLES

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124558
>>62124455
AHAHAHAHAHA ZIGGERNIGGERS COMPLETELY BTFO
Shekel just went vertical btw haha Jews won again.

62124572
>bought the top of bitcoin
>bought the top of silver
>bought the top of oil
bros I might be cursed

62124784
>>62124572
its a blessing. just inverse yourself in the future. im not even joking.

62124856
>>62124455
Yet spot prices are at $120+ and the oil supply is fucked for the rest of the year

Don't attempt to trade this shit unless you want to get rekt. The paper market for oil does its own thing and moves contrary to the market for physical oil. The price of futures is a function of massive hedging by trading firms and oil producers themselves as well as fuckery from the US government.

62127065
>>62124856
>Don't attempt to trade this shit unless you want to get rekt. The paper market for oil does its own thing and moves contrary to the market for physical oil.
Yeah I learned this the hard way the past few weeks. I didn't invest too much into commodities though, less than 5%.

62127188
>>62124856
No shit Sherlock. Do you not realize that futures are supposed to be hedges for oil companies?

62127216
>>62124856
have you tried being smarter?

62127217
>>62124455
only urals price in st.pethesburg is related

62127296
There are no oil baggies, retard

62127320
>>62127296
This senpai. I knew they'd essentially claim the war was over to drop the prices one last time before the ground invasion. Now I have more oil barrels than ever before at a lower cost basis.

62127383
>>62127296
>>62127320

62127393
>>62124455
So now that it's been confirmed that no ships went through the strait today and all the ones that tried u-turned... what now?

62128133
>mfw 50k in WTI June Oil futures

62128142
>>62127393
what now? what now?? well we wait for a Trump TRUTH post to tell us the truth. probably the truth is that those ships sailed smoothly, probably some of the smoothest sailing in years. the very best vessels

62128158
>>62127393
>>62128142
see >>62127383

62128783
uh oil bears what is this?

62128853
>>62124856
>The price of futures is a function of massive hedging
I feel like you do not even understand what purpose futures serve if you think this is some kind of revelation, kek

62128858
KEKAROO

62128878
>>62124572

62128879
>>62128853
Obviously futures are uses to hedge.

What I am saying is that the price of oil futures today is a function almost entirely of hedging strategies. It's being pushed around by major institutions using them in more complex moves. When you trade an oil future you are not betting on the future price of oil nor the movement in either direction. You are betting on price movement based on demand for paper contracts to be used in a more complex hedging strategy by major institutions. It's a completely different thing and you will get rekt if you think you can trade oil futures because you understand how the price of oil will move.

62128907
>>62124531
SYKE
whos the baggie now? Get ready for a rally on monday.

62128944
>>62128907

62129301
>>62124455
>>62128907
>>62128944
Kek, not so smug now, are we, OP?

62129336
>>62129301
>i want to pay more for gas
LOL

62129383
>Age of instant data transmission and info accessibility on a scale never seen before
>Somehow harder than ever before to figure out the truth and what is really happening
How did this come to pass?

62129397
>>62128879
its simple

>is oil prudction and distribution back to normal

if no then paper barrels prices go up

the reality of the situation hasn't even hit yet

62129411
>>62129383
Infinite data without context means you can never be sure of what's right and what's wrong.

62129425
>>62128944
This happens every time. It gets dumped by the treasury and then recovers. Only to get dumped even further. its been happening for weeks.

62129634
>>62129301
How do these people keep falling for it?
It's been almost 2 months of the exact same pattern of market manipulation and government lies.

62129709
>>62124455
WHAT???
are you telling me the infinite supply of nigger liquid is going DOWN IN VALUE???

item.title: Just scored myself 0.72 ETH browsing the blockchain What about you? — 11 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just scored myself 0.72 ETH browsing the blockchain What about you?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-04-19 00:46 UTC

OP

Just scored myself 0.72 ETH browsing the blockchain
What about you? Any big hauls lately?

Early replies (first 3)

62129855
>>62129817
How did u do it anon?

62130154
>>62129817
I bagged 0.2 unclaimed BTC on Friday, not a bad result!

62130163
>>62129855
I’ve made more ETH than that just passively staking on Coinbase. Not in one day but it adds up. The rewards come in like every 2-3 days.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62130166
>>62129855
he is lying m8

62130172
>>62129817
i have scored 43 doge

62130184
Someone left a whole BTC outside 711. Another great find!

62130185
>>62130172
>43 doge
lol what is that like $1?

62130186
>>62130185
its actually less lmao

62130192
>>62129817
I found a nigger living in the blockchain. And pedo stuff. It's not that easy to surf in the blockchain

62130348
>>62129817
Somebody did not wrap his Ethereum. Rooky mistake i found it laying around and now its mine.

62130409
>>62129817
Based anon, haven't seen one of these threads in awhile
